title: Container Image Build Time Trending
dataSources: `index=devops` `sourcetype IN ("gitlab:job","github:workflow_job","jenkins:build")` filtered to `image`/`container`/`docker` job or stage names
app: GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Jenkins Pipelines with Kaniko/buildkit logging
spl:
index=devops sourcetype IN ("gitlab:job","github:workflow_job") earliest=-90d@d
| eval job_lower=lower(coalesce(name,job_name,workflow_name))
| where match(job_lower,"(?i)image|container|docker|build.*push|kaniko|buildkit")
| eval dur_min=round(coalesce(duration_sec,duration)/60,2)
| eval sprint=strftime(_time,"%Y-W%V")
| stats avg(dur_min) as avg_build_min median(dur_min) as med_build_min by sprint, project
| eventstats median(avg_build_min) as fleet_med by sprint
| eval regression=if(avg_build_min > fleet_med*1.35,1,0)
| sort sprint project
description: Longer image builds slow every downstream deploy; sprint-level trends catch Dockerfile regressions, bloated layers, or registry latency before they dominate CI budgets.
value: Longer image builds slow every downstream deploy; sprint-level trends catch Dockerfile regressions, bloated layers, or registry latency before they dominate CI budgets.
implementation: Standardize job naming so filters stay reliable; alternatively maintain a lookup of pipeline IDs that produce images. Strip cache-hit jobs if duration is near-zero noise. Compare medians per repo against its own 8-sprint baseline to reduce cross-team skew. Pair with container registry pull latency dashboards when build times spike only in certain regions.
visualization: Line chart (average image build minutes by sprint), Bar chart (top regressing projects), Table (sprint, project, avg, median).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Jenkins Pipelines with Kaniko/buildkit logging.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=devops` `sourcetype IN ("gitlab:job","github:workflow_job","jenkins:build")` filtered to `image`/`container`/`docker` job or stage names.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Standardize job naming so filters stay reliable; alternatively maintain a lookup of pipeline IDs that produce images. Strip cache-hit jobs if duration is near-zero noise. Compare medians per repo against its own 8-sprint baseline to reduce cross-team skew. Pair with container registry pull latency dashboards when build times spike only in certain regions.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype IN ("gitlab:job","github:workflow_job") earliest=-90d@d
| eval job_lower=lower(coalesce(name,job_name,workflow_name))
| where match(job_lower,"(?i)image|container|docker|build.*push|kaniko|buildkit")
| eval dur_min=round(coalesce(duration_sec,duration)/60,2)
| eval sprint=strftime(_time,"%Y-W%V")
| stats avg(dur_min) as avg_build_min median(dur_min) as med_build_min by sprint, project
| eventstats median(avg_build_min) as fleet_med by sprint
| eval regression=if(avg_build_min > fleet_med*1.35,1,0)
| sort sprint project
```

Understanding this SPL

**Container Image Build Time Trending** ‚Äî Longer image builds slow every downstream deploy; sprint-level trends catch Dockerfile regressions, bloated layers, or registry latency before they dominate CI budgets.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=devops` `sourcetype IN ("gitlab:job","github:workflow_job","jenkins:build")` filtered to `image`/`container`/`docker` job or stage names. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Jenkins Pipelines with Kaniko/buildkit logging. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=devops, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **job_lower** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(job_lower,"(?i)image|container|docker|build.*push|kaniko|buildkit")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **dur_min** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **sprint** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sprint, project** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sprint** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **regression** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GitHub and GitLab audit or activity views for the same time range. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (average image build minutes by sprint), Bar chart (top regressing projects), Table (sprint, project, avg, median).
grandmaExplanation: We watch this part of your delivery chain for risks and reliability so issues show up before they hit customers.
knownFalsePositives: Trend moves with team size, release cadence, or company holidays; baseline per service line.
